The Origin Story

We solved a genuinely painful problem

Recruiters had three options for CV formatting — all slow, costly, or impractical. None scaled to the volume the industry demanded.

  • Do it manually — up to 20 minutes per CV, every single time
  • Send to a BPO or admin team — added cost and delay
  • Ask the jobseeker to reformat — friction and lost placements
  • Legacy parsers: $0.02–$0.03/doc at just 65–70% accuracy

Allsorter paired the original document with extracted data and highlighted exceptions — reducing effort from 20 minutes to 2.4 minutes per CV.

Time per résumé
Before Allsorter
20+ min
With Allsorter
2.4 min
88% reduction in effort

World's most advanced
HR data extraction

Allsorter's agentic stack sits at the zenith — purpose-built for HR data, delivering materially higher accuracy than legacy parsers or horizontal LLMs.

The Real Problem

The hidden "submission tax"

Formatting is only the visible part. The real bottleneck spans inputs, friction, context-switching, and lost selling time — every single day.

Inputs

  • CVs (PDF/DOC)
  • LinkedIn profiles
  • Job descriptions
  • Email attachments

Friction

  • Manual cleanup
  • Template mapping
  • Redaction
  • Re-keying into ATS

Lost Time

  • Delayed submissions
  • Lower differentiation
  • Lower conversion
  • Steals selling time

Winning Output

  • Client-ready profiles
  • Candidate intros
  • Shortlist packs
  • Structured ATS data

Insight: "The resume is only the container. The product is submission-ready output, fast."

Strategy 1 of 3

Win the longtail with frictionless, outcome-based pricing

Self-serve PLG for 160k+ agencies. Same simplicity as generic AI — but with recruitment-native workflow built in.

  • Price leadership with usage-based monetisation
  • Less user effort, more background automation
  • Recruitment-native workflow integration

Strategy 3 of 3 — Moonshot

Lead the next-gen HR LLM Intelligence layer for hiring

Build the first independent, multilingual, agentic submission platform for staffing — and the first agentic BPO model for candidate processing at global scale.

Phase 1
Intake & Parse
Universal résumé ingestion across all formats
Phase 2
Enrich & Rank
LLM skills normalisation and scoring
Phase 3
Route & Connect
Distribution to all five destination pools
Phase 4
Learn & Optimise
Feedback loop trains next model generation

Each phase compounds — more data → better enrichment → better routing → richer feedback signals.

Allsorter LLM intercepts, enriches, and redistributes global talent flow before it settles into siloed pools.

Owning the Intelligence Layer

4.5 billion résumés.
One intelligence layer.

The entity that controls the layer between jobseekers and destination pools controls the most valuable asset in talent acquisition: enriched, structured, real-time skills data.

4.5B
Annual résumé submissions flowing across five destination pools globally each year — volume accelerating
5
Fragmented destination pools — each siloed, capturing a unique slice of the same candidate population
1
Agentic intelligence layer — Allsorter LLM, the first cross-pool LLM built natively for hiring data flow
The Reset

From tool to infrastructure

Old Framing
"Resume formatting utility"
  • Point feature inside ATS workflows
  • Valued as a time-saver, not strategic
  • Competes on template breadth alone
  • Harder to expand beyond formatting
  • Perceived as replaceable by generic AI
New Framing
"Agentic submission infrastructure"
  • Own the workflow: Capture → Extract → Enrich → Generate → Verify → Submit
  • Distributed where recruiters work — ATS, Microsoft, Chrome
  • Sells outcomes: velocity, trust, operating leverage
  • Expands into shortlists & hiring-manager handoff
  • Competitors acquired by ATSs → clear gap for independence
Competitive Moats — 2026

Two defensible
moats

As competitors are acquired by ATSs, Allsorter's independence becomes its most valuable asset — the neutral layer that works across all systems.

Moat #1

Quality + Governance
Responsible AI
  • Configurable extraction pipelines
  • Confidence thresholds + explainability
  • Human-in-the-loop exception review
  • Multi-language processing (27 languages)

Moat #2

Neutral Infrastructure
System of action
  • Works across all ATS/CRM ecosystems
  • Complements Microsoft + Chrome workflows
  • Competitors acquired → clear gap for independence
  • Becomes "system of action" across all tools

Conclusion

"Traditional SaaS sells access to software.
Agentic platforms deliver outcomes."

Allsorter's opportunity is to reposition as the autonomous operating layer for recruitment document and submission workflows — dual-track, category-defining, and built for the future of hiring.

Appendix — Client Case Study

Transformation at scale

PageGroup's Scotland and Manila centres had 50 FTEs dedicated to CV processing — 16 minutes per CV across formatting, parsing and QC. At network scale: 7 million CVs per year.

Manila BPO (before)
  • 100+ people dedicated
  • 16 min per CV
  • Business hours only
Allsorter Agentic
  • 3 FTEs only
  • Near-instant processing
  • 24/7, scales to 7M CVs
